nodes.title: AI Detection Countermeasures
nodes.summary: Empirically tested techniques for reducing AI detection scores on generated text. Tested against GPTZero Premium (Model 2026-03-11-base) across 18+ attempts with sentence-level analysis.

nodes.title: Browser Automation
nodes.summary: Using headless or headed browsers programmatically to automate web interactions — clicking buttons, filling forms, navigating pages, extracting content. A recurring pattern across multiple projects.

nodes.title: Canvas Automation (Zion Work)
nodes.summary: Python utilities for reading and organizing assignments from Canvas LMS (SLA Beeber school instance). Uses Playwright for browser automation and the Canvas API for data access.

nodes.title: Claude API
nodes.summary: Anthropic's API for accessing Claude models. Used across multiple projects for classification, analysis, decision-making, and content generation.

nodes.title: Claude Code Agents

nodes.summary: Reusable instruction sets that tell Claude Code how to handle specific task types. Skills are loaded on-demand — their descriptions are always visible, but full content only loads when invoked. Thi...

nodes.summary: AI consultancy startup targeting faith-based nonprofits — Christian schools, churches, rescue missions, pregnancy centers, small NGOs. The pitch: save 10+ hours per week by upgrading communications...

nodes.title: Email Organizer
nodes.summary: Automates Gmail inbox management: fetches unread emails, classifies them as school or non-school using [[claude-api]], applies Gmail labels, and generates personalized draft replies that match your...

nodes.title: Ethan Hauger
nodes.summary: The person behind all these projects. High school senior at SLA Beeber in West Philadelphia.

nodes.title: File Structure Conventions
nodes.summary: The standard directory layout for projects using the [[wat-framework]]. Not all projects use every directory — if `tools/`, `workflows/`, or `tasks/` don't exist, those WAT instructions are skipped.

nodes.title: FireCrawl
nodes.summary: Headless browser scraping API. Renders JavaScript-heavy pages and returns clean HTML. Solves the problem of sites that block direct HTTP requests (403 errors).

nodes.title: Gemini API
nodes.summary: Google's LLM API. Used for conversational AI where the character needs to feel natural and responsive.

nodes.title: Gmail API
nodes.summary: Google's API for programmatic email access. Used for both reading (fetch, search) and writing (drafts, labels).

nodes.title: Groq API
nodes.summary: Fast, cheap LLM inference provider. Used when speed matters more than capability — quick decisions, transcription, and real-time interactions.

nodes.title: GSAP (GreenSock)
nodes.summary: Professional-grade JavaScript animation library. Used for timeline-based animations, scroll-triggered effects, and smooth transitions.

nodes.title: House Portfolio

nodes.title: LLM Routing

nodes.title: Mamie Smith Research
nodes.summary: School history research project exploring how Mamie Smith's 1920 recording of "Crazy Blues" contributed to Harlem becoming a national center for Black music during the Harlem Renaissance.

nodes.title: Movie Clone
nodes.summary: TMDB-powered movie and TV show browser built with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Features search, catalog browsing, detail pages with trailers, and dark/light theme switching.

nodes.title: MrBeast $1M Puzzle

nodes.title: Parallax Game

nodes.title: Playwright
nodes.summary: Browser automation library by Microsoft. Controls Chrome, Firefox, and WebKit programmatically. More reliable than Selenium, with built-in wait mechanisms and better selector support.

nodes.summary: The original poker bot — predecessor to [[poker-bot-v2]]. A more traditional architecture with separate agents, engine, executor, reader, and tracker modules. Uses a YAML config file rather than th...

nodes.title: PromptCraft

nodes.title: PyAutoGUI
nodes.summary: Python library for programmatic mouse and keyboard control. Operates at the OS level — clicks actual screen coordinates, types keystrokes, takes screenshots.

nodes.title: Scholarship Applications

nodes.title: School Assignments
nodes.summary: Comprehensive school assignment workflow built around [[ethan-hauger]]'s needs as a 12th grader at SLA Beeber. Combines writing style profiling, AI detection countermeasures, file generation skills...

nodes.title: Screen Reading & OCR
nodes.summary: Capturing screen regions and extracting text/data using OCR (Optical Character Recognition) and template matching. Used when there's no API — the screen IS the interface.

nodes.title: Scroll-Driven 3D

nodes.title: Self-Improvement Loop
nodes.summary: A core mechanism within the [[wat-framework]]. Every failure is treated as a chance to make the system stronger, not just something to fix and move on from.

nodes.summary: Roblox parkour hub where players race pre-built routes, duel-race friends, and build their own 60-second micro-courses that go live immediately for the whole server to run and rate. The Weekly Feat...

nodes.title: Student AI Toolkit
nodes.summary: Web application built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Details sparse — no CLAUDE.md. Appears to be a toolkit providing AI-powered tools for students.

nodes.title: Supabase
nodes.summary: Open-source Firebase alternative providing PostgreSQL database, auth, storage, and edge functions. Backend platform for web applications.

nodes.title: Tavily API
nodes.summary: AI-optimized search API for finding web content. Returns clean, relevant results filtered by source type (news, blogs, reddit, etc.). Designed for LLM applications.

nodes.title: The Lamppost

nodes.title: Three.js
nodes.summary: JavaScript 3D rendering library for creating WebGL experiences in the browser. The foundation of the [[house-portfolio]]'s interactive 3D environment.

nodes.title: WAT Framework
nodes.summary: The WAT framework (Workflows, Agents, Tools) is the core operating architecture used across all projects. It separates concerns so that probabilistic AI handles reasoning while deterministic code h...

nodes.title: Work Standards
nodes.summary: Operating principles that govern how the AI agent works within the [[wat-framework]]. These aren't suggestions — they're rules that apply across all projects.
